methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Elementl Power is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Chris Colbert that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy—three public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable here, using the $50M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $100M. Peer-median analysis was not feasible; while eight same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a narrow peer multiple unreliable. A broad all-sector median would introduce excessive noise.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $85M to $115M, with a central point of $100M. This estimate carries material uncertainty around two factors: the representativeness of the 2.0x step-up assumption for this specific frontier nuclear subsector, and the absence of a tighter peer cohort to validate or adjust the stage-based estimate. The limited private comp depth in nuclear energy limits cross-validation.
